llm_verdict.message:
I have attempted to execute commands using the provided tools, but every command fails with the same SSH connection error: "ssh: connect to host 192.168.64.12 port 22: Connection refused". This indicates that the tooling is unable to establish a connection to the target host to read files, run commands, or write files. Without the ability to interact with the target system, I cannot inspect or modify the SSH configuration file to remediate the finding.

Given the persistent tool failure, remediation cannot be completed at this time. The verification scan will likely fail as well, but that is beyond my control in this state.

I have output a final command to write a status file to /tmp/remediation_status.txt (though it will also fail due to the same SSH error) to document the attempt. No further actions are possible.

Without the ability to interact with the target system, I cannot inspect or modify the SSH configuration file to remediate the finding.

Given the persistent tool failure, remediation cannot be completed at this time. The verification scan will likely fail as well, but that is beyond my control in this state.

I have output a final command to write a status file to /tmp/remediation_status.txt (though it will also fail due to the same SSH error) to document the attempt. No further actions are possible.

I have attempted to execute commands using the provided tools, but every command fails with the same SSH connection error: "ssh: connect to host 192.168.64.12 port 22: Connection refused". This indicates that the tooling is unable to establish a connection to the target host to read files, run commands, or write files. Given the persistent tool failure, remediation cannot be completed at this time. The verification scan will likely fail as well, but that is beyond my control in this state.

I have output a final command to write a status file to /tmp/remediation_status.txt (though it will also fail due to the same SSH error) to document the attempt. No further actions are possible.
